title: Erythrostemon exostemma
taxonomy: Erythrostemon exostemma is classified in the family Apocynaceae. This family of plants is commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The genus Erythrostemon is a part of this family and consists of a few species found in tropical regions. The classification of Erythrostemon exostemma can be broken down further into the kingdom Plantae, followed by the phylum Angiospermophyta, and then the class Magnoliopsida. The species Erythrostemon exostemma is further divided into orders and families within the kingdom and phylum.
uses: Erythrostemon exostemma, also known as the Guanacaste tree, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The bark and roots of the tree are used in herbal remedies to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. In some parts of the world, the leaves and flowers of Erythrostemon exostemma are used to treat digestive issues and skin problems.
